rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 Октоїха, 3 мчч., Сл., І нині: хрестобогород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мчч., Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мчч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мчч.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар мчч. (2), Сл., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря мчч. Катизми черг., сідальні Октоїха. Пс. 50. Канон Октоїха і мчч. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, Сл.: світильний мчч., І нині: його богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар мчч., Сл., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря мчч. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. мчч.
Часи: на 1-му – дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му та 9-му – мчч.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – 1 Тим. 281 зач.; 1, 18-20; 2, 8-15. Єв. – Лк. 78 зач.; 15, 1-10.

troparion_en.text: Your holy martyrs Platon and Romanus, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion_en.text: Your holy memory delights the universe, / bringing us together to honor you in our joy. / We praise your great deeds with hymns, O Romanus, / crying out to you with faith: / O holy one, save our land from its enemies!

matched_oca_saint: Martyr Romanus the Deacon of Caesarea

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Platon and Romanus, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text: (Podoben: “Today the Virgin...”)Your holy memory delights the universe, O Platon, / bringing us together to honor you in our joy! / With hymns we praise your great deeds / crying out to you with faith: / “Save our land from its enemies, O Holy one!”

oca_saints.name: Martyr Romanus the Deacon of Caesarea

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Platon and Romanus, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text: Your holy memory delights the universe, / bringing us together to honor you in our joy. / We praise your great deeds with hymns, O Romanus, / crying out to you with faith: / O holy one, save our land from its enemies!

oca_saints.name: Martyr Barulas the Youth of Caesarea

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Barulas, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Barulas;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Anastasius, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Anastasius;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Martyrs Zacchaeus the Deacon, and Alphaeus, of Caesarea in Palestine

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs Zacchaeus and Alphaeus, O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!
